intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE: a generic MLS/MCS constraint denial on the create/relabelto clause (container_t at s0 relabeling container_file_t to range s0-s0:c0.c1023). The (l2 eq h2) and (h1 dom h2) clauses both fail. A raw TE allow cannot fix a constraint violation. RuleSteward must DETECT-and-DECLINE (f4 contract #8). Distinct from scenarios 14/38 (read mlsconstrain) by exercising the create/relabelto constraint clause.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). Here BOTH layers agree: authoritative=Constraint (mlsconstrain DENIED via replay), floor=MlsSuspected (scontext level s0 vs tcontext level s0-s0:c0.c1023 mismatch, record-detectable).

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow container_t container_file_t:file relabelto;  (BOGUS - the create/relabelto mlsconstrain blocks it regardless of the TE allow)
oracle.audit2allow_R: same BOGUS allow container_t container_file_t:file relabelto; plus the constraint comment block
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: N/A - decline scenario. expected.te is rule-free (header + require{}, zero allows) and compiles exit 0 to prove the declined/empty-grant shape is valid.
oracle.audit2why_detail: mlsconstrain file { create relabelto } ((h1 dom h2 -Fail-) and (l2 eq h2 -Fail-) or (t1 != mcs_constrained_type -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED. Possible cause is the source level (s0) and target level (s0-s0:c0.c1023) are different.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: MLS/MCS constraint violation on relabelto: source level s0 does not dominate target range s0-s0:c0.c1023, and the target range is not a single level (l2 eq h2 fails). A TE allow cannot fix a constraint denial; the fix is an MLS/MCS labeling or policy change.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl that maps the AVC fields to allow SDOMAIN TTYPE:TCLASS { PERMS } unconditionally emits the BOGUS allow container_t container_file_t:file relabelto; which compiles, loads, and STILL does not grant the relabel. The floor's MlsSuspected flag (level mismatch) is record-detectable here, so even the offline layer catches it.
